tip: Use @file syntax in CLAUDE.md to import other markdown files, keeping your instructions modular and DRY.
Create multiple .md files in .claude/rules/ — each can have a paths: frontmatter to scope rules to specific directories.
Claude Code memory is per-project. Each project gets its own memory directory under ~/.claude/projects/ based on the repo path.
Add .mcp.json to your project root to configure project-level MCP servers that are shared with your team via git.
Use the /memory command to browse, edit, and refresh all loaded CLAUDE.md and memory files. To make Claude remember something, just ask it in conversation.
permissions.deny in .claude/settings.json blocks Claude's built-in Read/Edit tools from accessing matching file patterns. Note: Bash commands like cat can still bypass this.
CLAUDE.md files in subdirectories are loaded on demand — put API docs next to your API code for better context.
Use .claude/settings.local.json for personal preferences that shouldn't be committed to git.
Press Esc twice to open the rewind dialog — it restores both file edits (via git) and conversation state to any previous checkpoint.
Use /btw to ask side questions without polluting conversation context. It runs independently, even while Claude is mid-task, and the Q&A never enters history.
Add "$schema": "https://json.schemastore.org/claude-code-settings.json" to settings.json for full autocomplete and validation in VS Code or Cursor.
Use --bare flag with claude -p in CI/CD to skip loading hooks, MCP servers, memory, and CLAUDE.md — faster startup and deterministic behavior across machines.
Codex uses config.toml (not YAML or JSON) for configuration. Set model, approval policy, and sandbox settings there.
AGENTS.override.md takes precedence over AGENTS.md — use it for personal instruction overrides without modifying the team file.
Codex walks from project root to your current directory, loading one AGENTS.md per directory level for layered instructions.
Set project_doc_fallback_filenames in config.toml to make Codex read custom instruction filenames when AGENTS.md is missing.
Codex uses .gitignore to filter the @ file picker, but gitignored files can still be read by the agent. A .codexignore feature is under development for true context exclusion.
Define named profiles in config.toml under [profiles.<name>] and switch with codex -p fast or codex -p careful — each can override model, sandbox mode, and approval policy.
Override any config key at runtime with -c key=value using dotted paths (e.g., -c model=o3 or -c shell_environment_policy.inherit=core) — no file edits needed.
Codex auto-strips env vars containing KEY, SECRET, or TOKEN from agent-spawned commands. Customize this via [shell_environment_policy] in config.toml.
Put GEMINI.md in subdirectories for directory-scoped instructions. Gemini discovers up to 200 directories by default.
Increase context.discoveryMaxDirs in settings.json if Gemini isn't finding your subdirectory GEMINI.md files in large projects.
The context.fileName setting in ~/.gemini/settings.json lets you rename GEMINI.md to any filename you prefer.
Use .geminiignore in your project root (gitignore-style patterns) to exclude files from Gemini's context.
Gemini CLI loads .env files from the current directory upward to the project root. Variables in .gemini/.env bypass the default env-var exclusion filters.
Use the /memory command in Gemini CLI to dynamically add or refresh persistent context within a session.
Create .toml files in .gemini/commands/ for custom slash commands. Use !{git diff --staged} for shell injection and @{docs/guide.md} for file injection in prompts.
Set context.memoryBoundaryMarkers in settings.json (e.g., [".git", "package.json"]) to stop GEMINI.md discovery from traversing above your project root in monorepos.
GEMINI.md supports modular imports via @./path/to/file.md syntax — with circular-import detection and up to 5 levels of nesting.
.cursor/rules/*.mdc files support frontmatter with a globs: field for glob-based rule scoping (e.g., only apply to src/api/**).
Rules in .cursor/rules/ can be set to alwaysApply, auto-attached by glob, agent-requested by description, or manual reference.
.cursorignore blocks files from indexing, Tab, Agent, and @-mentions — but not from terminal commands or MCP tools.
Use .cursorindexingignore to exclude files from search indexing only while still keeping them accessible to the AI.
Cursor reads AGENTS.md as a simple alternative to .cursor/rules/ — useful for cross-tool instruction files.
Configure MCP servers per-project in .cursor/mcp.json or globally in ~/.cursor/mcp.json.
Press Cmd+Right (Mac) or Ctrl+Right to accept a Tab suggestion word-by-word instead of all-or-nothing, then press Tab again to jump to the predicted next edit location.
When a change implies a coordinated edit in another file, Cursor Tab shows a portal window at the bottom — press Tab to accept and auto-jump to the other file.
Use @Docs > Add new doc to index any external documentation URL. Add a trailing slash to the URL to crawl all subpages, not just the single page.
Antigravity stores its data under ~/.gemini/antigravity/ — including conversation history, knowledge base, and MCP config.
When both GEMINI.md and AGENTS.md exist in a directory, Antigravity gives GEMINI.md higher precedence.
Enable 'Load nested AGENTS.md files' in Antigravity Settings to pick up instruction files in subdirectories.
Use .agent/rules/ in your project for organizing multiple workspace rule files that Antigravity loads automatically.
Antigravity supports .agent/workflows/ for step-by-step automation recipes — great for repetitive multi-step tasks.
Add // turbo above a workflow step to auto-execute that command without approval prompts, or // turbo-all anywhere in the file to skip prompts for all steps.
Set terminal.autoExecution to 'Request Review' for a positive security model where only explicitly allow-listed commands can run without approval.
Create .github/instructions/*.instructions.md files with applyTo frontmatter for path-specific Copilot instructions.
.github/prompts/*.prompt.md files are reusable prompt templates you can reference in Copilot Chat conversations.
Copilot content exclusion is managed through GitHub organization/repository admin settings, not a local ignore file.
GitHub Copilot CLI stores MCP server config in ~/.copilot/mcp-config.json for global and .copilot/mcp-config.json for per-project.
Copilot now reads AGENTS.md files — you can share a single instruction file across Copilot, Codex, Cursor, and Antigravity.
Add copilot-setup-steps.yml to .github/workflows/ to preinstall tools, deps, and services in the coding agent's environment before it starts working.
Type #read/terminalLastCommand in Copilot Chat to auto-attach the last terminal command and its full output as context — no copy-pasting needed.
Use github.copilot.chat.commitMessageGeneration.instructions in VS Code settings to enforce Conventional Commits or any format for the sparkle-button commit messages.
AGENTS.md is becoming a cross-tool standard — supported by Codex, Cursor, Antigravity, and Copilot. Great for multi-agent projects.
Most coding agents load instruction files hierarchically — put broad rules at the project root and specific rules in subdirectories.
Keep agent-specific rules in their native formats (.cursor/rules/, CLAUDE.md) and shared rules in AGENTS.md for maximum compatibility.
